audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot tabernacle construction text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Sabbath reminder, willing-hearted offerings, wise-hearted artisans, women spinning, Bezalel/Oholiab teaching and craftsmanship.

verses.restored: Moses assembled all congregation of children of Israel and said to them, These are the matters that YHWH commanded to do them.
Six days work shall be done, but on seventh day there shall be for you holiness, Sabbath of Sabbath-rest to YHWH; everyone doing work on it shall be put to death.
You shall not kindle fire in all your dwellings on Sabbath day.
Moses said to all congregation of children of Israel, saying, This is the matter that YHWH commanded, saying,
Take from among you a contribution to YHWH; everyone willing of heart shall bring it, YHWH's contribution: gold, silver, bronze,
blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen, goats' hair,
reddened ram skins, durable hides, acacia wood,
oil for light, spices for anointing oil and fragrant incense,
onyx stones and stones for setting, for ephod and breastpiece.
Every wise-hearted one among you shall come and make all that YHWH commanded:
dwelling-place, its tent and covering, clasps, frames, bars, pillars, and bases;
ark and its poles, cover, and veil of screen;
table and its poles and all its vessels, and bread of presence/faces;
lampstand for light, its vessels and lamps, and oil for light;
incense altar and its poles, anointing oil and fragrant incense, and screen of entrance at entrance of dwelling-place;
altar of burnt offering with its bronze grating, poles, and all its vessels, basin and its stand;
hangings of court, its pillars and bases, and screen for gate of court;
pegs of dwelling-place, pegs of court, and their cords;
woven garments for ministering in holy place, holy garments for Aaron the priest, and garments of his sons to serve as priests.
All congregation of children of Israel went out from before Moses.
They came, every person whose heart lifted one, and everyone whose spirit made one willing; they brought YHWH's contribution for work of tent of meeting, for all its service, and for holy garments.
They came, men along with women, everyone willing of heart; they brought brooch, earring, ring, and ornament, every gold vessel, and everyone who waved wave offering of gold to YHWH.
Everyone with whom was found blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen, goats' hair, reddened ram skins, and durable hides brought them.
Everyone contributing contribution of silver and bronze brought YHWH's contribution; and everyone with whom was found acacia wood for any work of service brought it.
Every wise-hearted woman spun with her hands, and they brought what they spun: blue, purple, scarlet, and fine linen.
All women whose heart lifted them in wisdom spun goats' hair.
The leaders brought onyx stones and stones for setting, for ephod and breastpiece,
and spice and oil for light, anointing oil, and fragrant incense.
Every man and woman whose heart made them willing to bring for all work that YHWH commanded by hand of Moses—the children of Israel brought freewill offering to YHWH.
Moses said to children of Israel, See, YHWH called by name Bezalel son of Uri son of Hur, of tribe of Judah.
He filled him with Spirit of Divine, in wisdom, understanding, knowledge, and every craft,
to devise designs, to work in gold, silver, and bronze,
in cutting stones for setting and in carving wood, to make in every skilled craft.
And he put in his heart to teach, he and Oholiab son of Ahisamach of tribe of Dan.
He filled them with wisdom of heart to do every work of engraver, designer, embroiderer in blue, purple, scarlet, and fine linen, and weaver, doers of every work and devisers of designs.

translation_notes: The construction section begins with Sabbath: even holy work is bounded by rest.
Contribution is freewill and willing-hearted, not coercive collection.
Women are explicitly wise-hearted artisan contributors, spinning linen and goats' hair.
Bezalel and Oholiab are not only artisans but teachers; craft wisdom is transmissible Spirit-gift.
safety_note: Do not use the Sabbath penalty as modern coercive practice. The governing frame is that sacred work itself submits to rest, and holy making arises from willing hearts and wise craft.
